identity.name: Telegram Logger Bot (System)
identity.purpose: A modular system for monitoring, filtering, and logging Telegram communications. Consists of a bot application and an isolated persistence module.

component.name: Application Layer (Root)
component.file: Main entry point. Implements aiogram Dispatcher, event handlers, and business logic. Orchestrates Dependency Injection by instantiating DatabaseRepository and HistoryCollector. Responsible for Access Control (filtering) and logging both incoming and outgoing data.
Configuration manager. Loads environment variables, processes ACLs (ALLOWED_USERS, ALLOWED_CHATS), and normalizes Telegram IDs.
Maintenance utility. Exports logged data to JSON or HTML by utilizing the DatabaseRepository directly.

component.name: Persistence Layer (Module)
component.folder: Isolated module specialized in parsing Telegram objects and SQLite persistence. Utilizes Dependency Injection for repository management. Refer to 'tg_bot_history/documentation.ai.xml' for detailed module specs.

access_control.logic: 1. Check if chat_id is in ALLOWED_CHATS or user_id (for private) is in ALLOWED_USERS. 2. If unauthorized in a group: send a farewell message and leave the chat. 3. If unauthorized in private: ignore silently.

logic_flows.integration_flow: 1. 'bot.py' initializes 'DatabaseRepository' with DB_PATH. 2. 'bot.py' injects the repository instance into 'HistoryCollector'. 3. Upon receiving/sending updates, 'bot.py' validates the source against ACLs. 4. Validated updates are passed to 'HistoryCollector' for parsing and persistence via the injected repository.

maintenance_and_tools.export: The 'dump_db.py' tool provides a relational view of the captured data. It is used for human-readable audits (HTML) and data migration (JSON).
maintenance_and_tools.deployment: Requires a .env file with BOT_TOKEN and DB_PATH. The system uses SQLite WAL mode to allow the dump tool to read while the bot is running.

future_expansion.note: The system is designed to provide structured history context for Large Language Models (LLM). Future 'ContextProviders' will consume the same 'DatabaseRepository' instance to build LLM prompts.
